segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: Hi Coleman, how are you?
segments.zh: 嗨，科尔曼，你好吗？

segments.speaker: COLEMAN
segments.en: Good, thanks.
segments.zh: 挺好的，谢谢。

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: I wanted to have a chat with you because our friend Josh told me that you've joined a guitar group and it sounds interesting.
segments.zh: 我想跟你聊聊，因为咱们的朋友乔希告诉我你加入了一个吉他小组，听起来挺有意思的。我自己也很想学。你为什么不一起来呢？我肯定还有空位。

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: I'd really like to learn myself.

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: Why don't you come along?

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: I'm sure there's room for another person.

segments.speaker: COLEMAN
segments.en: Really?
segments.zh: 真的吗？那——谁负责上课？

segments.speaker: COLEMAN
segments.en: So – who runs the classes?

segments.speaker: COLEMAN
segments.en: He's called a 'coordinator' – his name's Gary Mathieson.
segments.zh: 他被称为‘协调员’——名字叫加里·马西森。

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: Let me note that down.
segments.zh: 我记一下。加里……他的姓怎么拼？

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: Gary. . . .

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: How do you spell his surname?

segments.speaker: COLEMAN
segments.en: It's M-A-T-H-I-E-S-O-N.
segments.zh: 是M-A-T-H-I-E-S-O-N。

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: Right, thanks.
segments.zh: 好的，谢谢。

segments.speaker: COLEMAN
segments.en: He's retired, actually, but he's a really nice guy and he used to play in a lot of bands.
segments.zh: 他其实已经退休了，但他是个特别好的人，以前在很多乐队里演奏过。

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: Thanks.
segments.zh: 谢谢。那你去了多久了？

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: So how long have you been going?

segments.speaker: COLEMAN
segments.en: About a month now.
segments.zh: 大概一个月了。

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: And could you play anything before you started?
segments.zh: 你开始之前会弹什么吗？

segments.speaker: COLEMAN
segments.en: I knew a few chords, but that's all.
segments.zh: 我会几个和弦，但仅此而已。

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: I'm sure everyone will be better than me.
segments.zh: 我肯定每个人都比我弹得好。

segments.speaker: COLEMAN
segments.en: That's what I thought, too.
segments.zh: 我当时也这么想。我第一次和加里通电话时，他说这是初级班，但我还是担心每个人都比我强，结果我们都一样差劲！

segments.speaker: COLEMAN
segments.en: When I first spoke to Gary on the phone, he said it was a class for beginners, but I was still worried that everyone would be better than me, but we were all equally hopeless!

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: Oh, that's reassuring.
segments.zh: 哦，那让人放心了。那你们在哪里见面？

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: So where do you meet?

segments.speaker: COLEMAN
segments.en: Well, when I joined the group, they were meeting in Gary's home, but as the group got bigger, he decided to book a room at the college in town.
segments.zh: 嗯，我刚加入的时候，大家是在加里家里碰面的，但随着人越来越多，他决定在镇上的学院订个房间。我更喜欢去那儿。

segments.speaker: COLEMAN
segments.en: I prefer going there.

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: I know that place.
segments.zh: 我知道那个地方。我上中学的时候去过那儿上踢踏舞课。不过后来再没去过，也记不清在哪条街了……是洛克街吗？

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: I used to go to tap dancing classes there when I was at secondary school.

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: I haven't been since, though and I can't remember what road it's in . . . is it Lock Street?

segments.speaker: COLEMAN
segments.en: It's just beyond there at the bottom of New Street near the city roundabout.
segments.zh: 就在那附近，新街尽头靠近城市环岛的地方。

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: Yes, of course.
segments.zh: 哦，对。

segments.speaker: COLEMAN
segments.en: The guitar club is on the first floor in Room T347.
segments.zh: 吉他俱乐部在三楼，T347房间。

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: Right.
segments.zh: 好的。你们什么时候碰面？周末吗？

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: And when do you meet?

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: Is it at the weekend?

segments.speaker: COLEMAN
segments.en: We meet on Thursdays.
segments.zh: 我们周四碰面。以前是十点半，我觉得挺合适的，但现在改到十一点了。因为之前用那个房间的班级要求多占用半小时。

segments.speaker: COLEMAN
segments.en: It used to be 10.30 and that suited me well, but now we meet at 11.

segments.speaker: COLEMAN
segments.en: The class that's in there before us asked if they could have the room for another 30 minutes.

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: Oh, I see.
segments.zh: 哦，这样啊。嗯，我很想去，但我没有吉他。

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: Well, I'd love to come, but I don't have a guitar.

segments.speaker: COLEMAN
segments.en: Well, you can always buy a second-hand one.
segments.zh: 嗯，你总可以买个二手的。有个叫‘完美乐器’的网站，卖各种吉他、小提琴之类的。我相信你肯定能在那里找到合适的。

segments.speaker: COLEMAN
segments.en: There's a website called 'The perfect instrument' that sells all kinds of guitars, violins and so on.

segments.speaker: COLEMAN
segments.en: I'm sure you'll find something there.

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: So what's a typical lesson like with Gary?
segments.zh: 那么加里的课一般是什么样的？

segments.speaker: COLEMAN
segments.en: Well, he always starts by getting us to tune our guitars.
segments.zh: 嗯，他总是先让我们调音。大概要花五分钟。

segments.speaker: COLEMAN
segments.en: That takes about five minutes.

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: Uhuh.
segments.zh: 嗯哼。

segments.speaker: COLEMAN
segments.en: Some people have an app they use, but others do it by ear.
segments.zh: 有些人用手机应用，但其他人靠耳朵听。加里会走过去帮他们。同时，他会告诉我们这节课要做什么。

segments.speaker: COLEMAN
segments.en: Gary goes round and helps them.

segments.speaker: COLEMAN
segments.en: And while he's doing that, he tells us what he's going to do during the lesson.

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: Right.
segments.zh: 好的。

segments.speaker: COLEMAN
segments.en: First, we usually spend about ten minutes doing some strumming.
segments.zh: 首先，我们通常花十分钟左右练习扫弦。

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: So is that using . . . what are they called . . . plectrums?
segments.zh: 那是用……叫什么来着……拨片吗？

segments.speaker: COLEMAN
segments.en: No – we just use our thumbs.
segments.zh: 不——我们就用大拇指。

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: Much easier.
segments.zh: 那简单多了。

segments.speaker: COLEMAN
segments.en: Gary reminds us where to put our fingers for each chord and then we play them together.
segments.zh: 加里会提醒我们每个和弦手指放哪儿，然后我们一起弹。有时候我们笑得停不下来，因为节奏太差了，加里就会拍手帮我们找拍子。

segments.speaker: COLEMAN
segments.en: Sometimes we all just start laughing because we're so bad at keeping time, so Gary starts clapping to help us.

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: Do you learn to play any songs?
segments.zh: 你们会学弹歌曲吗？

segments.speaker: COLEMAN
segments.en: Yes – we do at least one song with words and chords.
segments.zh: 会的——我们至少学一首带歌词和和弦的歌。我的意思是，那比你想的难。

segments.speaker: COLEMAN
segments.en: I mean that's harder than you think.

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: Oh, I'm sure it is!
segments.zh: 哦，那肯定的！

segments.speaker: COLEMAN
segments.en: That part of the lesson takes about 15 minutes.
segments.zh: 那部分课大概15分钟。他经常先放一首歌的录音给我们听，然后发下歌谱，如果有新和弦，我们就先练那个，再一起弹——但速度很慢。

segments.speaker: COLEMAN
segments.en: He often brings a recording of the song and plays it to us first.

segments.speaker: COLEMAN
segments.en: Then he hands out the song and if there's a new chord in it, we practise that before we play it together – but really slowly.

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: Do you do any finger picking?
segments.zh: 你们练指弹吗？

items.prompt: Complete the form below. Write ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.

pairs.note: 题干要求填写名字，原文直接给出，拼写相同。

paraphrase.explain: 题干要求填写姓名，原文中'his name's Gary Mathieson'直接给出了答案。

pairs.note: 题干问课程级别，原文说这是为初学者（beginners）的课，直接对应。

paraphrase.explain: 题干关键词'Level'在原文中被具体化为'class for beginners'，直接定位到答案'beginners'。

pairs.note: 原文直接出现 college，与答案一致。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到 'book a room at the college in town'，其中 college 即为题干询问的地点。

pairs.note: 原文直接出现'New Street'，题干只取'Street'，但答案需要完整名称。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'New Street'，题干只给出'Street'，需要听出前面的'New'。

pairs.note: Thursday morning 对应 Thursdays，表示每周四上午。
介词 at 直接对应，表示时间点。
数字 11 直接对应，是答案。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问周四上午几点，原文说现在在11点见面，直接定位到数字11。

pairs.note: 题干与原文完全一致，直接定位网站名称。

paraphrase.explain: 题干直接引用网站名称'The perfect'，原文中完整出现'The perfect instrument'，通过匹配网站名称即可定位答案。

pairs.note: 题干用动名词短语，原文用动词短语，意思相同。
介词by直接对应，表示方式。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问的是通过app或什么方式，原文中'by ear'直接对应'by'后的内容，但答案'ear'在原文中紧跟在'by'后，所以定位到'by ear'即可找到答案。

items.prompt: keeping time while the teacher is

pairs.note: 原文直接出现，帮助定位
老师Gary通过拍手来帮助保持节奏

paraphrase.explain: 题干问老师做什么来帮助保持时间，定位句提到Gary开始拍手，因此答案clapping。

pairs.note: 题干说'经常听'，原文说'经常带来录音'，动作主体不同但意思相近。
题干中的'a'对应原文中的'a recording'，是冠词与名词的搭配。

paraphrase.explain: 题干中的'often listening to a'与原文中的'often brings a recording'对应，其中'often'直接匹配，'listening to'被替换为'brings'，'a'后接'recording'，因此答案词'recording'来自原文。
